components.role: Filter resistor — sets cutoff frequency with C1 as fc = 1/(2*pi*R1*C1)
Filter capacitor — sets cutoff frequency with R1

spice_template:
R1 in out {{R1}}
C1 out gnd {{C1}}

dsp_model.description: First-order IIR lowpass filter; -3dB at cutoff, -20dB/decade rolloff above

behavioral_description: The simplest possible tone control — a single resistor and capacitor that progressively rolls off treble above the cutoff frequency. The rolloff is gentle at -6dB per octave (first order), so it darkens the tone without creating a sharp frequency cliff. This is the basic building block found in guitar tone knobs, post-distortion tone shaping, and as a treble-cut filter after clipping stages to tame harshness. At low cutoff frequencies (1-2kHz) it produces a dark, muffled 'woman tone'; at higher settings (5-8kHz) it takes off just the fizzy top end while preserving presence and clarity.
